seo.title: Payroll Company Jamaica 2026: How to Choose the Right Payroll Service
seo.description: Looking for a payroll company in Jamaica? Compare payroll services, outsourcing vs software, costs, and what Jamaican businesses really need to stay TAJ-compliant in 2026.

title: Payroll Company Jamaica 2026: How to Choose the Right Payroll Service
subtitle: The Complete Guide for Jamaican Business Owners Comparing Payroll Companies, Services, and Software

ctaInline.headline: Skip the middleman — handle payroll yourself in minutes

ctaBottom.headline: Jamaica's payroll company built into software

html:
Do You Need a Payroll Company or Payroll Software?

When Jamaican business owners search for a "payroll company in Jamaica," they are usually looking for one of two things:

  1. A payroll outsourcing firm — a company that handles your entire payroll process on your behalf
  2. Payroll software — a tool you use yourself to process payroll accurately and file returns with TAJ

Both options solve the payroll problem. The right choice depends on your business size, how much control you want, and your budget. This guide helps you make the right decision.

What a Payroll Company in Jamaica Actually Does

A traditional payroll company (outsourcing firm) in Jamaica will typically:

  • Collect timesheets and attendance data from you each pay period
  • Calculate gross pay, PAYE, NIS, NHT, and Education Tax for each employee
  • Generate payslips and deliver them to your staff
  • File your monthly S01 returns with TAJ on your behalf
  • Remit statutory contributions to NIS Jamaica and NHT Jamaica
  • Provide annual P6 forms and year-end reports

For this service, you pay a monthly fee — typically per employee per month — which can range from JMD $3,000 to JMD $8,000 per employee depending on the complexity of your payroll.

The True Cost of Outsourcing to a Payroll Company in Jamaica

Outsourcing payroll in Jamaica often costs more than business owners expect. Here is a realistic cost breakdown:

Business SizeOutsourced Payroll Cost/MonthSoftware Cost/MonthAnnual Saving with Software
5 employeesJMD $20,000–$35,000JMD $5,000–$8,000JMD $144,000–$324,000
15 employeesJMD $45,000–$90,000JMD $12,000–$20,000JMD $396,000–$840,000
50 employeesJMD $120,000–$250,000JMD $35,000–$60,000JMD $1,020,000–$2,280,000

The savings compound over time. What many businesses discover is that payroll software gives them full control and the same level of compliance as a payroll company — at a fraction of the cost.

What to Look for When Evaluating a Jamaica Payroll Company

If you decide outsourced payroll is right for your business, evaluate providers on these criteria:

1. Jamaica Tax Knowledge

Any payroll company operating in Jamaica must demonstrate current knowledge of:

  • PAYE threshold and tax rates (updated each budget cycle)
  • NIS insurable earnings ceiling
  • NHT calculation methodology
  • Education Tax rates
  • TAJ S01 filing requirements and deadlines

Ask prospective providers: "What was the PAYE threshold for the 2025/2026 tax year?" A legitimate Jamaica payroll company will answer JMD $1,500,096 immediately.

2. TAJ Filing Responsibility

Clarify in writing: who is responsible if a filing is late or incorrect? Some outsourcing firms pass penalties back to the client. A quality payroll company assumes responsibility for errors caused by their calculations.

3. Data Security

Your payroll data contains employee TRNs, NIS numbers, salary information, and bank account details — some of the most sensitive data your business holds. Confirm:

  • Where is data stored? (On-island servers vs. overseas)
  • Who has access to employee salary information?
  • What happens to your data if you stop using the service?

4. Turnaround Time

How quickly does the payroll company process a payroll run once they receive your data? For monthly payroll, you need payslips ready by the last working day of the month. For weekly payroll, the turnaround must be measured in hours, not days.

5. Self-Service Access

Can your employees access their own payslips online? Modern payroll companies provide employee portals where staff can download payslips, view year-to-date earnings, and update personal information. This reduces HR admin significantly.

When to Use Payroll Software Instead of a Payroll Company

Payroll software is the better choice when:

  • You have fewer than 100 employees and want to control costs
  • You process payroll more than once a month (payroll companies charge per run)
  • You want real-time visibility into payroll costs for budget planning
  • You have variable pay (commissions, overtime, bonuses) that changes each period
  • You want the ability to run ad-hoc payslips or make corrections immediately
  • You are growing quickly and your payroll complexity is increasing

When Outsourcing to a Payroll Company Makes Sense

Payroll outsourcing works well when:

  • You have a large, stable payroll (100+ employees, fixed monthly pay)
  • Your HR team lacks payroll expertise and training is not feasible
  • You are dealing with complex multi-jurisdiction payroll (Jamaica + overseas staff)
  • You want fully managed compliance including audit defense and TAJ correspondence
  • You have a significant enough budget that the cost is not a concern

Jamaica Payroll Compliance Requirements — What Any Provider Must Handle

Whether you use a payroll company or software, these Jamaica statutory requirements must be met every month:

Monthly Filings (Due by the 14th)

  • S01 Return (TAJ) — Summary of all PAYE, Education Tax contributions for the month
  • NIS Monthly Return — Total NIS contributions (employee + employer) by employee
  • NHT Monthly Contribution — Total NHT contributions remitted to NHT Jamaica

Annual Filings (Due by April 14)

  • P6 Forms — Annual earnings and deduction summary for each employee
  • Annual Employer Return (TAJ) — Reconciliation of all payroll tax remitted during the year

Employee Requirements

  • Issue payslips with every payment showing gross pay, all deductions, and net pay
  • Provide P6 form to each employee by April 14 after tax year end
  • Maintain payroll records for minimum 7 years (TAJ audit requirement)

Red Flags When Evaluating Jamaica Payroll Companies

Watch out for payroll providers who:

  • Cannot explain the difference between NIS and NHT calculations
  • Do not provide written service level agreements
  • Cannot produce sample payslips that show all statutory deductions
  • Are not familiar with the TAJ S01 filing process
  • Cannot confirm their liability for filing errors
  • Ask you to sign over Power of Attorney for TAJ filings without explanation

PayrollJamaica: The Software That Replaces a Payroll Company

PayrollJamaica is built by Jamaicans for Jamaican businesses. Unlike outsourcing your payroll to a third party, our software puts you in control while automating the complex parts:

  • PAYE, NIS, NHT, Education Tax — all calculated automatically for every employee
  • S01 return generated automatically — ready to file with TAJ
  • Employee payslips generated in one click, accessible via employee portal
  • Compliant payroll records kept for 7+ years
  • Budget-updated rates pushed automatically after every Jamaican Budget presentation

Most Jamaican businesses that switch to PayrollJamaica from a payroll company recoup their annual software cost in the first two months of savings.

Getting Started

If you are currently using a payroll company and want to evaluate whether software makes sense for your business, the easiest way is to try PayrollJamaica free for one payroll cycle. Enter your employee data, run a test payroll, and compare the output against what your current provider produces. If the numbers match — and they will — you will know the software can handle your payroll accurately.

The switch from a payroll company to software takes less than a day of setup time for most Jamaican businesses. We provide onboarding support and a migration guide to make the transition seamless.
